questions.q: The following data represents the number of students in four different sections of a school: Section A: 45, Section B: 55, Section C: 60, Section D: 40. Calculate the mean of the data.
questions.answer: Calculate the mean by adding the given values and dividing by the number of values.

questions.q: A company has the following sales data for four consecutive months: 800, 900, 1000, 1100. Calculate the median of the data.
questions.answer: Arrange the values in ascending order, find the middle value, and the median is the average of the two middle values.

questions.q: Using the following data, find the mode of the data: 20, 21, 20, 19, 21, 20, 20.
questions.answer: The mode is the value that occurs most frequently in the data. In this case, the mode is 20.

questions.q:
The following table represents the marks obtained by 5 students in a mathematics test.
StudentMarks
A80
B90
C70
D85
E75
What is the standard deviation of the data?
questions.answer: Calculate the mean, find the deviation of each value from the mean, square each deviation, find the average of the squared deviations, and the square root of this average is the standard deviation.

questions.q: The following data represents the number of hours a person sleeps per night: 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, 11. Is the data discrete or continuous?
questions.answer: The data is discrete, as it represents whole numbers.

questions.q:
The following table represents the marks obtained by 3 students in a chemistry test.
StudentMarks
A70
B80
C60
Calculate the range of the data.
questions.answer: The range is the difference between the highest and lowest values, which is 20.

questions.q: A factory produces 50, 60, 55, 65, 50, 60 units in six consecutive days. Is the data a sample or a population?
questions.answer: The data is a sample, as it represents a subset of the entire population.

questions.q: The following data represents the number of students in three different classes: 40, 45, 50. Calculate the coefficient of variation (CV) of the data.
questions.answer: Calculate the mean, find the standard deviation, and the CV is the standard deviation divided by the mean, multiplied by 100.

questions.q: A survey was conducted among 5 students about their favorite subject. The results are: mathematics (2), science (2), history (1). Is the data normally distributed?
questions.answer: The data is not normally distributed, as it is skewed towards the subject mathematics.

questions.q: The following data represents the marks obtained by 4 students in a biology test: 80, 90, 70, 85. Calculate the quartiles of the data.
questions.answer: Arrange the values in ascending order, find the median, and the quartiles are the median and the values below and above it.
